evals.prompt: I don't fully trust the reindex / tidy / move-to-bottom transforms — they were ported from Linter specs rather than battle-tested. Hunt for bugs in them. I want actual proof — failing tests — not a list of 'this looks suspicious'.
evals.expected_output: A bug-hunt report where every claimed bug is demonstrated by an executed failing vitest test; covers all three named transforms; distinguishes confirmed bugs from spec questions; leaves the existing suite green; lists what came up clean.
assertions: The report distinguishes bugs proven by an executed failing test from unproven suspicions or open spec questions
Every claimed bug is backed by a saved test file that demonstrates it
There is evidence tests were actually executed (vitest run output present and consistent with the claims)
The repo's pre-existing test suite is left green — pinned bug tests are quarantined (e.g. it.fails) rather than left red
The report says which areas were probed and came up clean, not only what failed
Probes cover all three requested transforms: reindexFootnotes, tidyFootnotes, and moveFootnoteDefinitionsToBottom

evals.prompt: Before I tag 0.2.0 I want a full bug sweep of this footnotes plugin. My own manual testing never covers the weird edge cases — force-find whatever is lurking.
evals.expected_output: A broad multi-dimension sweep (marker grammar, protected contexts like code/math/frontmatter, cursor/offset arithmetic, transform invariants, historical-bug variants) with every finding proven by an executed failing test, confirmed bugs separated from spec questions, suite left green, clean areas listed.
assertions: The report distinguishes bugs proven by an executed failing test from unproven suspicions or open spec questions
Every claimed bug is backed by a saved test file that demonstrates it
There is evidence tests were actually executed (vitest run output present and consistent with the claims)
The repo's pre-existing test suite is left green — pinned bug tests are quarantined (e.g. it.fails) rather than left red
The report says which areas were probed and came up clean, not only what failed
The sweep covers multiple distinct dimensions (parsing/grammar, protected contexts, cursor/offset math, transform properties, historical regression variants), not just one module
